'''Leon Hayes''' (born 4 March 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[West Wales Raiders]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]] and at [[North Wales Crusaders]] in 2023<ref>{{Cite web |date=20 Sep 2023 |title=Crusaders' coach Carl praises the impact of talented Hayes |url=https://www.leaderlive.co.uk/sport/23803027.crusaders-coach-carl-praises-impact-talented-hayes/ |access-date=24 Sep 2023 |website=Leader Live}}</ref>

In 2022 Hayes made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Wire]] in the [[Super League]] against the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62661403 Super League: Huddersfield Giants 38-36 Warrington Wolves - Matt Dufty scores four tries as Wire comeback falls short]</ref>

In 2024 Hayes made his breakthrough playing in Round 2 due to an injured [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|George Williams]], once Williams returned Hayes was favoured to play in the halves alongside him over [[Josh Drinkwater]]. He was selected every game following by new Head Coach [[Sam Burgess]]. Hayes played only two games for Warrington in the 2025 Super League season as they missed the playoffs finishing 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC (loan)===
On 9 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Apr 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Leon Hayes among 7 Warrington Wolves youngsters to seal Championship loan deals|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-loans-09-04-2026}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (loan)===
On 16 April 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Widnes Vikings RLFC|date=16 Apr 2026|accessdate=19 Apr 2026|title=Half-back Leon Hayes arrives on loan|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/vikings-sign-leon-hayes/}}</ref>
